nodes.name: Fetch ACLED Events
nodes.notes: IMPORTANT: replace /path/to/meridian with your actual project path before running. This calls acled_fetch.py, which reads ACLED_EMAIL/ACLED_PASSWORD from the .env file via python-dotenv — n8n itself never sees these credentials.

nodes.name: Severity >= 6?
nodes.notes: Splits the pipeline: high-severity events (>= 6, roughly battles/violence against civilians with casualties) get routed to immediate-attention handling; everything still flows to general storage.

nodes.name: Store All Events (configure your DB)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER: Replace this with your actual storage node — e.g. Supabase 'Insert Row', Postgres node, or another Execute Command calling a storage script. Left as a no-op so the workflow is importable and runnable end-to-end before you've set up Supabase/Postgres.

nodes.name: Flag for Review (configure notification)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER: Replace this with a notification node (Slack, Email, Telegram) to alert you when a high-severity event lands. This is the human-in-the-loop gate from MERIDIAN's design — high-severity events shouldn't wait for your next scheduled review.

meta.templateNote: MERIDIAN ACLED ingestion module. Before running: (1) replace /path/to/meridian in the Execute Command nodes with your real path, (2) ensure .env is set up per docs/acled_setup.md, (3) replace the two NoOp placeholder nodes with your actual storage and notification integrations.
